claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 17 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 17 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances and the children in your care.

Let me start with the first question: **Do you have a child or children that you're responsible for?** If yes, could you please tell me how many children you're asking about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do. I'm asking about my son Alex - he's 17 years old and lives with me at home.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I'm assessing eligibility for your son Alex, who is 17 years old and lives with you.

Since Alex is 17, I need to ask: **Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** 

This would include things like:
- A-levels or similar qualifications
- T-levels
- Scottish Highers
- NVQs up to level 3
- Home education (if started before they turned 16)
- Certain training courses

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he is. Alex is in sixth form doing his A-levels at the moment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you. Since Alex is doing A-levels in sixth form, that counts as approved education.

Now I need to ask: **When did Alex turn 16, or when will he turn 16?** (I need to know the specific date if possible, or at least the month and year)

This is important because there are specific rules about how long Child Benefit can continue after a child turns 16 while they're in education.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He turned 16 last year. He's 17 now, so he's been in sixth form for a while already.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. To be more specific, could you tell me **which month and year Alex turned 16?** 

Also, I need to know: **Do you know when his current education or training is scheduled to end?** (For example, when does his A-level course finish?)

These dates are important because Child Benefit for 16-19 year olds in education has specific time limits.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I don't have the exact dates off the top of my head right now - I'd need to check his birth certificate for the precise date he turned 16, and the college would have his course end date. Is that information absolutely necessary for you to tell me if I'm eligible in general?

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's a fair question. Let me explain what I can tell you at this stage:

**In general terms**, you can continue to get Child Benefit for a child aged 16-19 if they're in approved full-time education or training (which Alex is, doing A-levels). However, the benefit stops on specific dates that depend on when they turned 16 and when their course ends.

Before I can give you a definitive answer about your eligibility, I do need to ask a few more general questions:

1. **Are you (or your partner if you have one) responsible for Alex?** This means you're supporting him financially and he lives with you.

2. **Do you or your partner currently live in the UK?**

Let me check these basic eligibility criteria first, and then we can discuss whether the specific dates are needed for a final determination.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I'm responsible for Alex - he lives with me and I support him. And yes, I live in the UK, in Leeds actually.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. And just to check: **Do you have a partner, and if so, are they also living in the UK?**

Also, I need to ask: **Is anyone else claiming Child Benefit for Alex, or is anyone else claiming he lives with them?** (For example, Alex's other parent, if applicable)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't have a partner - it's just me and Alex. And no, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him. It's just the two of us at home.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Let me summarize what you've told me about your situation:

- You're responsible for Alex (17 years old)
- He lives with you in Leeds
- He's in sixth form doing A-levels (approved education)
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him
- You're living in the UK
- You don't have a partner

**Based on the eligibility rules, you appear to be eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

The key criteria are met:
- You're responsible for a child under 20
- The child is in approved full-time education (A-levels)
- You live in the UK
- No one else is claiming for him

**Important notes:**
- Child Benefit normally stops when a young person turns 20, or when they leave approved education or training (whichever comes first)
- It also stops on 31 August (or the Monday after the last Tuesday in May/June if they leave education then) following their 16th birthday if they're not continuing in education
- Since Alex is 17 and currently in A-levels, your benefit should continue until he finishes his course or turns 20

Would you like any information about how to claim, or do you have any other questions?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's brilliant, thank you so much for explaining everything! That's really helpful to know I'm eligible. I appreciate you walking me through all of that.
